title: Caramelized Floral Fish Fillet Stir-fry
description: A floral, crisp, indulgent Portuguese dish featuring low-fat-friendly fish fillets that are sautéed with a blend of aromatic herbs and spices to complement their natural flavors.

ingredients.name: Sriramurthy or similar low-sodium soy sauce
Low-fat friendly yogurt or sour cream for serving (optional)

instructions: Clean and pat dry the fish fillets.
Heat olive oil in a non-stick skillet over medium heat. Add onion, garlic, and herbs; stir for about 1 minute until onions soften.
Add fish fillets to the pan, skin-side down first, reduce heat to low-medium, and cook for approximately 5-6 minutes per side or until cooked through (internal temperature should reach 70°C).
Drizzle Sriramurthy over the fish. Add water or broth in a small amount, just enough to create a thin sauce and caramelize.
Cover with aluminum foil and bake for about 10 minutes, then remove the foil for another few minutes to enhance caramelization if desired.
Serve hot with a dollop of low-fat friendly yogurt or sour cream on top as a creamy finish.
